---
title: "NBA Scorigami Center"

execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false

format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: false
---
## Interactive Frequency Map
::: {.glass-card}

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(viridis)

# Read data
games <- read_csv("cleaned_games.csv")

# Create heatmap data
score_matrix <- games %>%
  count(home_pts, visitor_pts)


p <- ggplot(score_matrix, aes(
  x = home_pts,
  y = visitor_pts,
  fill = n,
  text = paste0(
    "Home: ", home_pts,
    "<br>Visitor: ", visitor_pts,
    "<br>Games: ", n
  )
)) +
  geom_tile(color="#111111", linewidth=.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option="inferno", name="Game Count") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(20,190,20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(20,190,20)) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    subtitle="The greater mass of games sits slightly below the 45-degree line, highlighting the home team advantage.",
    x="Home Points",
    y="Visitor Points"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=12) +
  theme(

    panel.background=element_rect(fill="transparent",color=NA),

    plot.background=element_rect(fill="transparent",color=NA),

    legend.background=element_rect(fill="transparent"),

    legend.box.background=element_rect(fill="transparent"),

    panel.grid.major=element_line(color="#444444"),

    panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),

    axis.text=element_text(color="white"),

    axis.title=element_text(color="white"),

    plot.subtitle=element_text(color="white"),

    legend.title=element_text(color="white"),

    legend.text=element_text(color="white")
  )

ggplotly(p, tooltip="text") |>
layout(
  paper_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)",
  plot_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)"
)
```

:::

::: {.glass-card}

## Key Insights

**Most games cluster between 90–120 points per team.**

The bright orange region near the center represents the scoring range where NBA games most commonly finish. This reflects the typical offensive output across modern NBA seasons.

**The diagonal represents close games.**

Scores near the diagonal indicate competitive games. The brightest region sits close to this line because most NBA games are decided by relatively small margins.

**Blowouts are uncommon.**

As you move farther from the diagonal, games become increasingly rare. Large victory margins occur much less frequently than close contests.

**Extreme scores are outliers.**

The isolated squares in the corners represent historically unusual games—either extremely low-scoring games from earlier eras or exceptionally high-scoring games caused by overtime or unusually fast-paced offenses.

:::

::: {.glass-card}

## Did You Know?

- 🏀 The NBA has recorded tens of thousands of games since the 1946–47 inaugural season.

- ⏱️ Overtime games account for many of the unusually high scores in the upper-right corner.

- 📈 Because points are scored in one-, two-, and three-point increments, certain final scores occur far more frequently than others.

- ⭐ A **Scorigami** occurs when an NBA game finishes with a score that has never happened before.

Hover over the visualization above to explore every score combination and discover which final scores are common—and which are nearly unique.

:::
